tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57921,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Commit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now: - **If the default API version is GA** → only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded) - **If the default API version is itself a preview** → all versions (GA + preview) are listed The generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior. ## Gaps Found - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` — The "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section says *"Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order"* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview. - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` — Section 6 "Output Types" describes `ResourceVersions` only as *"nested class with GA API version constants"*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included. - Neither document explains what constitutes a "preview" version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic — version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.). 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` Edit the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section. Locate the paragraph: ```markdown Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order: ``` Replace it with: ```markdown Lists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource: - **When the default version is GA** — only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out. - **When the default version is itself a preview** — all versions (GA and preview) are included. A version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive). ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` In Section 6 "Output Types" → "Resource Provider" bullet list, locate: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants ``` Replace with: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain. ### Step 3: Verify README structure These are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check). No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24122942156/agentic_workflow) · ● 961.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57921","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z"}

When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain. ### Step 3: Verify README structure These are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check). No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24122942156/agentic_workflow) · ● 961.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57921","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z"}

{"total_count":23,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4139581423,"number":57489,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] ARM resource common properties should be emitted before other properties","body":"## Description

The new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator emits ARM resource properties in a different order than the old reflection-based generator. Specifically, `properties` is emitted before `name` and `location`, which changes the Bicep output ordering.

### Expected behavior

The old reflection-based generator sorted properties with this ordering logic (from `Specification.Analyze.cs`):
```csharp
resource.Properties.OrderBy(p => (p.Name == "Name" ? 0 : p.IsReadOnly ? '3' : p.IsRequired ? '1' : '2') + p.Name)
```

This ensured `name` was always first, followed by required properties alphabetically, then read-only/output properties.

Generated `DefineProvisionableProperties()`:
```csharp
_name = DefineProperty<string>("Name", ["name"], isRequired: true);
_location = DefineProperty<AzureLocation>("Location", ["location"], isRequired: true);
_properties = DefineModelProperty<KeyVaultProperties>("Properties", ["properties"], isRequired: true);
```

### Actual behavior

The new generator emits:
```csharp
_properties = DefineModelProperty<KeyVaultProperties>(nameof(Properties), new string[] { "properties" }, isRequired: true);
_name = DefineProperty<string>(nameof(Name), new string[] { "name" }, isRequired: true);
_location = DefineProperty<AzureLocation>(nameof(Location), new string[] { "location" }, isRequired: true);
```

Since the Bicep emitter uses insertion order, this causes `properties:` to appear before `name:` in the Bicep output.

### Impact

All regenerated provisioning libraries will have a different Bicep property ordering than before. While functionally equivalent, this is a behavioral change that breaks existing tests and differs from standard Bicep conventions where `name` and `location` typically appear before `properties`.

### Proposed fix

The provisioning generator should ensure ARM resource common properties (`name`, `location`, `tags`) are always defined before the `properties` bag and other custom properties in `DefineProvisionableProperties()`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues that relate to code generation","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTA4NDExMTc3"},{"id":6560170331,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Provisioning","name":"Provisioning","color":"ffeb77","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABhwRBWw"}],"comments":0,"closed_at":"2026-03-27T08:18:56Z","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z","updated_at":"2026-03-27T08:18:56Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM72vP_v"},{"id":4139580056,"number":57488,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Child resource location property incorrectly marked as required instead of output-only","body":"## Description

The provisioning generator incorrectly marks `location` as `isRequired: true` for child resources (e.g., `KeyVaultSecret`) when the TypeSpec definition has `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` (read-only) and is optional.

### Expected behavior

For `Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets`, the TypeSpec definition is:
```typespec
@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)
location?: string;
```

The old reflection-based generator correctly generated:
```csharp
_location = DefineProperty<AzureLocation>("Location", ["location"], isOutput: true);
```

### Actual behavior

The new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator generates:
```csharp
_location = DefineProperty<AzureLocation>(nameof(Location), new string[] { "location" }, isRequired: true);
```

This causes `location: location` to appear in the Bicep output for child resources, which is incorrect — the [Bicep reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.keyvault/vaults/secrets?pivots=deployment-language-bicep) confirms `location` is not a settable property on `vaults/secrets`.

### TypeSpec reference

https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/[REDACTED]/specification/keyvault/resource-manager/Microsoft.KeyVault/KeyVault/Secret.tsp

### Impact

Any provisioning library regenerated with the new generator will emit spurious `location` properties for child resources.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues Milestone: Migrate existing provisioning libraries to TypeSpec-based generator","body":"## Overview

Tracking the migration of all existing provisioning libraries from the reflection-based generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

Only provisioning libraries whose peer management library has already been migrated to TypeSpec (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) are eligible for migration. Additionally, the provisioning generator targets **stable (GA) API versions only**, so libraries whose TypeSpec spec only defines preview versions are blocked until a stable version is added.

## API Version Status

| Library | Mgmt API Version | Spec Has Stable? | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault | `2026-02-01` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** |
| Azure.Provisioning.Communication | `2026-03-18` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** |
| Azure.Provisioning.ContainerService | `2026-01-01` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** |
| Azure.Provisioning.AppConfiguration | `2025-06-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |
| Azure.Provisioning.EventHubs | `2025-05-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |
| Azure.Provisioning.Kubernetes | `2025-12-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |
| Azure.Provisioning.RedisEnterprise | `2025-08-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |
| Azure.Provisioning.SignalR | `2025-01-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |
| Azure.Provisioning.WebPubSub | `2025-08-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked |

## Sub-issues

- [ ] #56820
- [ ] #57382
- [x] #57383
- [ ] #56747
- [ ] #57384
- [ ] #56821
- [ ] #57385
- [ ] #56822
- [ ] #57386

## Shared Generator Issues

These generator issues affect multiple provisioning libraries and must be resolved to unblock migrations:

- #56751 - Mgmt `NameVisitor` property renames not applied
- #56752 - Missing `[DataMember]` attribute on enum values
- #56744 - Generate BuiltInRole types and CreateRoleAssignment methods
- #56753 - Generate custom action methods (e.g., GetKeys)
- #56745 - `ResourceVersions` should include all historical GA API versions
- #56743 - Missing `GetResourceNameRequirements()` override

## Related

- Provisioning generator tracking: #56738
- Provisioning generator PR: Generator] dotnet build /t:GenerateCode fails due to missing transitive dependency","body":"## Description

`dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` fails for provisioning SDK packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`) because the published provisioning emitter package is missing `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager` as a dependency.

## Error

```
Cannot find package '@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager' imported from
.../node_modules/@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt/dist/emitter/resolve-arm-resources-converter.js
```

Followed by TypeSpec compilation errors (`Unknown identifier ResourceNameParameter`, `Unknown decorator @armResourceOperations`, etc.)

## Root Cause

The provisioning emitter package.json (`eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json`) only declares:

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning": "1.0.0-alpha.20260316.3"
  }
}
```

The provisioning emitter transitively depends on `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, which imports `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager` in `resolve-arm-resources-converter.js`. However, this transitive dependency is not resolved when `tsp-client update` runs `npm ci` in the `TempTypeSpecFiles` directory.

## Workaround

Use `RegenSdkLocal.ps1` instead of `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`:

```powershell
pwsh eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/RegenSdkLocal.ps1 -Services "KeyVault"
```

This works because it builds the emitter locally with all dependencies available.

## Expected Behavior

`dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` should work for provisioning SDK packages, just as it works for management SDK packages.

## Found In

Provisioning Migration - KeyVault arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies a documentation gap in the provisioning generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`) — specifically, the `ResourceVersions` behavior around conditional preview API version filtering is not reflected in `provisioning-library-spec.md` or `design.md`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (provisioning generator in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`)
**Affected API:** `ResourceVersions` nested class / `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`
**Scenarios:**
- When the default API version is GA, only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews excluded)
- When the default API version is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are listed
- The `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic (strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`) is undocumented

**Root ask:** Update `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` and `docs/design.md` to document the conditional logic governing which API versions are included in `ResourceVersions`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The behavior change was introduced in commit 21456f8 (PR #57862). The generated test files `ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs` and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` already reflect the new behavior; only the documentation lags behind.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs` to confirm the `IsPreviewApiVersion` logic and how `ResourceVersions` is populated.
2. Update the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section in `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` to describe both the GA-default and preview-default behaviors.
3. Update Section 6 "Output Types" in `docs/design.md` to reflect the conditional version inclusion logic.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — The issue is about the provisioning generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`) which generates `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries.
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — The affected files are under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`, which is a code generator package per the label rules.
- **Confidence:** High — Identical label combination (`Provisioning` + `CodeGen`) confirmed by multiple similar reference issues: #57489, #57488, #57165, all filed against the provisioning generator in `eng/packages/`.
</details>
